sanskrit: न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय । उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्मसु ॥
hindiTranslation: हे धनञ्जय  उन सृष्टिरचना आदि कर्मोंमें अनासक्त और उदासीनकी तरह रहते हुए मेरेको वे कर्म नहीं बाँधते ॥
englishTranslation: Those actions of creation do not bind me, Dhananjaya, because I remain detached and indifferent to them.
essence: Action can continue without leaving a chain behind.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Creation and world-order do not bind Krishna, because he remains unattached and unconcerned while they happen.
simpleInsight: You can act in the world without being trapped by it.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna hears Krishna explain how the cosmos itself unfolds. After describing repeated creation and dissolution, Krishna adds that these actions do not bind him, because he stands apart from them, unattached while they continue.
modernRelevance: You send the message, make the hard call, and still do not let the role swallow you. The action happens; your identity does not have to cling to it.
translationLiteral: न च (and not) / माम् (me) / तानि (those) / कर्माणि (actions) / निबध्नन्ति (bind) / धनञ्जय (Dhananjaya) / उदासीनवत् (like one indifferent) / आसीनम् (remaining) / असक्तम् (unattached) / तेषु (in those) / कर्मसु (actions)
